metadata.description: time: - `time.time()` timestamp. - `time.sleep()` pause. - `time.localtime`, `time.mktime`, `time.strptime`. Python code examples: ```python import time # Example of using time.time() to get the current timestamp current_timestamp = time.time() print("Current Timestamp:", current_timestamp) # Example of using time.sleep() to pause execution for 5 seconds print("Pausing for 5 seconds...") time.sleep(5) print("Resume execution") # Example of converting a string to a struct_time object and back timestamp = time.time() struct_time_obj = time.localtime(timestamp) print("Local Time:", struct_time_obj) string_time = "2023-10-04 12:00:00" time_struct = time.strptime(string_time, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S") print("Time Struct:", time_struct) ``` These examples demonstrate the usage of `time.time()`, `time.sleep()`, and conversions between string and struct_time using `time.strptime()` and `time.localtime()`.
